name: follows
title: Follows
description: A directed follower -> following edge between two accounts. A faithful port of the source system's Follow model, which used a database unique_together(follower, following) constraint. This package does not reconstruct that constraint server-side (no per-collection uniqueness check exists in this codebase's schema validation): the follow button in objects/site/profile.py checks for an existing edge client-side before creating one, but a determined caller of the raw records API could still create a duplicate row. "storage": "append" because this is a write-hot, never-edited edge log -- same shape as app-catalog's stock_moves and app-thread's thread_comments (docs/append-only-storage-design.md). Unfollow is a delete of the edge row, not a state flag -- the simplest thing that matches the source's directed-edge semantics without inventing a tombstone convention this package doesn't otherwise need.

fields.name: owner_id
fields.label: Owner
fields.help: Equal to follower_id: the follower owns the edge they created, so they alone can delete it (unfollow). The account being followed has no write access to someone else's follow row.
